rag_rules: Place implementation-level bindings in the target implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use assign statements only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in the implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not invent new declarations; use only visible identifiers from the current component scope
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type contracts in the component type scope and implementation contracts in the implementation scope
Use exact visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not invent new declarations
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings
Use `guarantee "label": expression;` for output dependency contracts in type scope
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not invent new declarations; use only visible identifiers from the current component scope
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
